publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-20 00:10:44 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Records of the Methodist Episcopal Church of Ovid, N.Y. including histories of the church written in 1868, 1877, 1878 and 1880; lists of probationers, class members, members, pastors, baptisms, marriages and other church statistics; and a secretary's book of the church's Christian Endeavor Society. Records of the First Presbyterian Church of Ovid including session meeting minutes (1810-1832, 1832-1864); lists of members, baptisms, marriages, and deaths; and trustee records (1849-1897). Also records of the Federated Church in Ovid, formed in 1956 containing trustee records (1956-1965) and membership information.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: Before the Methodist Episcopal Church was built and opened in 1828-29, several different circuit preachers served the church organization in Ovid. Prior to 1820, Ovid was a charge on the Seneca circuit, but in 1820 it appears in the General Assembly records as a distinct appointment served by Rev. William Fowler. He formed the first class in the same year, consisting of eight people, one of whom was a black women named Lydia Demond. The Methodist Church at Sheldrake was under the same administration until about 1887 (not represented in this collection). Similarly, in 1836 and 1837 Ovid and Trumansburg villages united on the Seneca cir-cuit as a single charge. Until 1858-59 the Methodist Episcopal Church at Ovid was in the Geneva District, but was moved to the Elmira District shortly afterward, then moved back to Geneva in 1860-61, and moved yet again to Elmira in 1863. In 1876 the church went into debt $9,010, but managed to pay it off by October 1877.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST

emph.ref: Series I. Methodist Episcopal Church of Ovid, N.Y.

arrangement.p: Subseries A.: Book I 1. History, covering 1793-1880.
Subseries B.: Book II 1. History Cont., 1868-1880 (Note: should be 1868-1905).
Subseries C.: Book III 1. Christian Endeavor Society a. A Secretary's Book, 1919-1924 (Note: should be 1919-1948).
Subseries D.: Book IV 1. Funerals and Marriages, 1945-1951.
Subseries E.: Book V 1. Membership Roll, 1946-1956.
Subseries F.: Book VI 1. Membership cont., 1956-1977.

emph.ref: Series II. Presbyterian Church of Ovid, New York. (see Item 2 below)

arrangement.p: Subseries A.: Book I 1. History, volume I, 1803-1832.
Subseries B.: Book II 1. History, volume II, 1832-1862 (Note: should be 1832-1864).
Subseries C.: Book III 1. Trustee's Book, 1849-1897.
Subseries D.: Book IV 1. Trustee's Book, 1894-1946.
Subseries E.: Book V 1. Trustee's Book, 1846-1950.(Note: should be 1946-1957). 2. Trustee's Book, 1950-1956.(Missing).
Subseries F.: Book VI 1. Trustee's Book of the Ovid Federated Church, Methodist-Presbyterian, 1956-1965.
Subseries H.: Book VIII 1. Membership cont., 1864-1902.
Subseries I.: Book IX 1. Membership cont., 1887-1945.
Subseries J.: Book X 1. Membership cont., Baptisms, 1889-1931.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Federated Church of Ovid records, #6036. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Records of the Methodist Episcopal Church of Ovid, N.Y. including histories of the church written in 1868, 1877, 1878 and 1880; lists of probationers, class members, members, pastors, baptisms, marriages and other church statistics; and a secretary's book of the church's Christian Endeavor Society. Records of the First Presbyterian Church of Ovid including session meeting minutes (1810-1832, 1832-1864); lists of members, baptisms, marriages, and deaths; and trustee records (1849-1897). Also records of the Federated Church in Ovid, formed in 1956 containing trustee records (1956-1965) and membership information.

controlaccess.geogname: Seneca County (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs.
controlaccess.subject: Church records and registers -- New York (State) -- Ovid.
Presbyterian Church -- Membership.
Methodist Episcopal Church -- New York (State) -- Ovid.
Presbyterian Church -- New York (State) -- Ovid.
Federated churches -- New York (State) -- Ovid.

did.unittitle: Methodist Episcopal Church of Ovid, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written by M.S. Leet on April 17, 1868. He recorded the names of circuit preachers and the years they served in Ovid, revivals and who led them, the changes in Ovid's position as part of either the Geneva or Elmira District, and Ovid's position in one circuit or another. There were many revival uring the period 1829-1868.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By J.W. Steele, dated Sept. 15, 1871. entry is about the conflict and controversy between the church administration and the congregation over the $9,010 debt against the new church and parsonage uildings (begun in 1867, as recorded in part 1 above). They were able to regain he buildings after selling the mortgages, and paid the debt by acquiring new members and increased subscriptions after a protracted meeting and revival.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This entry records the decision of a quarterly conference meeting held September 25, 1878 to strike a sentence in J.W. Steele's previous entry from the record. Signed by Manly S. [Hard], presiding Elder of the Elmira District, March 5, 1880.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This entry records the entire process of paying off the debt. Written by Calvin L. Connell, September 21, 1880.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This records a resolution of the Quarterly Conference to thank those who worked to pay off the debt. It is signed by Calvin L. Connell, September 22, 1880

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Names, state in life, residence, when received, by whom, class joined, when and how baptized, when and how disposed of.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Class number, leader, when meets; names, state in life, residence, remarks.

did.unittitle: 8. Alphabetical Record of Members in Full Connection, 1863-1880

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Names, state in life, when received, how received, by whom, class number, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Name of officer, office held, who appointed, when removed, how removed, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pastor's name, when appointed, when removed, estimated salary, salary received; Members: white or colored; Probationers: white or colored, deaths, local probationers; Baptisms: adult or child; Church Property: value and amount spent on improvements; Benevolent Contributions: conference claimants, missions, Bibles, Tract., Sunday School Union, miscellaneous; Sunday School Statistics: schools, officers, teachers, scholars volumes in library, Bible classes, expenses of school, Sunday School advocates taken, conversions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: date, names of parties, occupation of male, ages, officiating minister, remarks.

did.unittitle: 1. History, by Calvin L. Connell, dated Sept. 20, 1880.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists names of contributors and amounts of contributions to the church.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Same information as in Book I.

did.unittitle: 4. Alphabetical Record of Members in Full Connection, 1877-1898

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Same information as in Book I.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Same information as in Book I.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Same information as in Book I.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Same information as in Book I.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Same information as in Book I.

did.unittitle: Book III: Christian Endeavor Society. Secretary's Book, 1919-1948.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alphabetical list of members, four pages.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In them are recorded date, committee reports, election of officers, social functions organized and collections taken.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In alphabetical order with dates and names.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists family name, occupation of family head, name of same, address, full name, when and how received, other members in family, birth date, date of baptism, church school and change in status.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists family name, Christian name, father and mother's full names, date and place of birth, adult/child/infant, place of baptism, officiating minister and change in church relationsip.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists number, name, address, manner of reception, date of enrollment and disposition of membership.

did.unittitle: First Presbyterian Church of Ovid, New York. Records.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: History: In 1800 Rev. John Linsey organized a Presbyterian Church in Ovid while on a four month mission for the General Assembly. The church came under the Oneida Presbytery, but was soon after moved to the Geneva Presbytery. It requested dismissal from this organization sometime in 1802 to join the classis of the Reformed Dutch Church. In 1803, the Oneida Presbytery issued a commission for the organization of a church in Ovid, and in the same year Jedediah Chapman organized the First Presbyterian Church of Ovid, consisting of 20 members. From 1803 to 1817 it was called the "Seneca Church." It is probable that the First Presbyterian Church had succession in the "True Reformed Dutch Church" of Ovid.
Scope: The First Presbyterian Church records in this part of the collection (1803-1856) are in Part II Books 1-V in the table of contents. From 1803-1817 known as The Seneca Church of Ovid.

did.unittitle: 1. "Records of The Seneca Church of Ovid"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Confession of faith; covenant of faith members: male and female.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records elections of officers; admissions and dismissals of members.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: when received, names, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: date, names of children and parents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: no names or information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: no heading. Shows dates, names, and residences.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records, p. 5. Catalogue of Members, p. 232. Catalogue of Baptisms, p. 264. Catalogue of Dismissions, p. 200.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: no author.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: records admissions and dismissals, some hearings conducted on charges made by church members against other members (for example, a long hearing recorded on pp. 70-79 dated Aug. 9, 1844).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Amended List of 1858: when received, names, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: date, name, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: date, name, parents' names, child or adult.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: date, name, where removed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Meeting minutes record appointments of officials, financial information on church property, collection of subscriptions and pew rent and how to get people to pay subscriptions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In them are recorded date, members present, committee reports, election of trustees, resolutions passed and budget concerns. Last entry is dated August 2, 1946.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: years elected and removed.

did.unittitle: 3. Will of John H. Sutton, n.d.
4. Treasurer's report of The First Presbyterian Women's Association, n.d.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes begin January 16, 1950. Content same as Book IV, with an emphasis on church property maintenance. Last entry is dated January 7, 1857.

did.unittitle: Federated Church of Ovid, New York. Records.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: History: In 1956, the boards of trustees of the Methodist Church and the First Presbyterian Church joined to form the Federated Church of Ovid.

Scope: The records i this part of the collection (1803-1965) are books VI through X. Book VI is a trustee's book (1956-1965) of the Federated Church, Book VII is a Membership Register (1803-1869), and Books VIII, IX and X are also membership registers (n.d.), all for the First Presbyterian Church of Ovid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titled "Minutes of Trustees and Finance Committee of the Federated Church of Ovid to be called from here on 'Federated Board of Trustees.'" Contents same as Books IV and V in Item Two. The following items are of note:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes begin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Last entry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists of trustees.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Calendar dates,", typed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists date of admission, name, wife or widow of, how received, when baptized, married and remarks. Note: 1803-1810 all information except names is unknown. The following items are of note:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 added on examination.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 33 added on examination.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 17 added on examination.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 21 added on examination.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contents same as Book VII. The following items are of note:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Directions for making entries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Index to contents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alphabetical list of members: gives name and page reference.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chronological Register (membership lists): gives names of resident members, when and how received, when baptized, wife or widow of, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 47 added on examination.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 26 added on examination.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Deaths and dismissions: gives name, date removed or died, where removed. (2 pages)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Infant baptism: lists date, name, birthdate, parents' names, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marriages: lists date, names, witnesses, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Elders: records date chosen, name, date of ordination, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Deacons: records date chosen, name, date ordained, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pastors: lists date installed, names, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Deacons: as above. [Note: pages missing between 331 and 336.]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The following items are of note:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Class record: lists number, names, time of class, location.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alphabetical record of members in full connection: lists name, state in life, when and how received, by whom received, when and how removed, class number, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probationers: lists name, state in life, when received, by whom received, when baptized, class number, when and how disposed of.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marriages: lists date, name and residence of man, name and residence of woman, officiating minister, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Baptisms: lists date, name, adult or infant, parents' names, birthdate, place of baptisms, officiating minister, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The following items are of note:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pastors: lists name, when installed, dismissed or died.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Elders: lists name, when ordained/installed, removed or died.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Deacons: same as above (one page).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Communicants: lists name, date and manner received, when and how removed, died, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marriages: lists name and residence of man, same for woman, date, witnesses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Deaths: lists name, date, remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Certificate of baptism.
